history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible up and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The cell to my right is open floor, so the best next step is to rotate clockwise toward that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly in front at row 06 column D is a safe floor cell. The goal is visible up and to the left, while the left-adjacent path is blocked by lava at row 07 column C. Moving forward is the safest step and keeps progress toward reaching the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible two rows ahead and two columns to the left of my current position. I am facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is empty floor and safe to enter. Moving forward brings me closer to the goal while avoiding the lava to the upper-left path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is immediately to my left, but the cell directly in front of me is lava and the cell to my right is a wall. Since I am facing upward, I should turn left so that I can face the goal and move toward it on the next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible two rows ahead and aligned with the column immediately to my left: from my current position at row 07 column D, the goal is at row 05 column D. The cell directly in front of me is lava at row 06 column D, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my left is safe floor at row 07 column C and provides a path toward the goal through row 06 column C and row 05 column D. I should turn left first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible on my current row, two cells to the right of my position. The cell directly in front of me is free floor, while the cell to my right is lava, so I should not turn toward the goal immediately. Moving forward is safe and positions me to approach the goal from a safer route.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. The goal is not visible, and moving forward is safe and explores more of the map, while turning is unnecessary.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with a wall directly to my left, lava directly to my right, and open space not visible ahead because it is outside the shown corridor. The row in front of me is unknown, but staying aligned with the open corridor is preferable to turning into lava or a wall. Moving forward is the only safe exploratory action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with a wall directly to my left and open cells to my right side, but the cell directly in front of me is unknown. Since the visible corridor on my current row is blocked on the left and lava is immediately to my right, moving forward into the unexplored cell is the best safe progress toward discovering a path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with a wall directly to my left and open cells to my right side, while the cell directly ahead is unknown. The goal is not visible. Moving forward explores safely, whereas turning right would face a lava cell immediately in front. So the best next step is to advance into the unknown cell ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with a wall directly to my left at row 07 column C, lava directly to my right at row 07 column E, and the cell in front of me at row 06 column D is a wall. Moving forward is blocked, so I need to rotate. Since the right side leads toward lava, I will turn left to avoid facing the dangerous side first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. The open floor cells are to my left at row 07 columns B and C, while the cell to my right is also a wall. To reach the open path, I should turn left first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible up and to the left of my position. I am facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. Moving forward is safe and brings me closer to the row of the goal. Turning now is unnecessary, and moving left immediately is impossible without first repositioning and rotating. I will advance one step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor cell. The goal is visible at row 06 column B, but there is lava at row 06 column C blocking a direct leftward approach from ahead. Moving forward is safe and may let me reposition toward the goal, while turning is unnecessary right now.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The goal is visible immediately to my left at row 07 column B, but there is lava at row 07 column C between me and the goal, and a wall directly to my right at row 07 column E. The cell in front of me at row 06 column D is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. The only sensible safe action is to turn left or right; turning left begins reorienting toward the open area away from the blocked front and right side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>
